title: Seed default agent_presets via a new doctor guided fix
action:
Per D1 (docs/history/seed-agent-presets-legacy-config/CONTEXT.md), add a new doctor check + guided fix so operators with a valid config whose agent_presets list is empty can seed it without running `herdr-go update`. All in src/doctor/checks.rs:

1. In build_checks(), inside the `if let Some(c) = &config { ... }` block that already runs the 'allowed roots' check (around line 202-220), add one more check with label "agent presets": if `c.agent_presets.is_empty()`, push `Check::info("agent presets", "none configured yet -- the '+' sheet only shows Shell")`; otherwise push `Check::info("agent presets", format!("{} preset(s) configured", c.agent_presets.len()))`. Per D4 this MUST be Check::info (ok:true) -- never Check::fail/critical:true, nothing is broken.

2. Add a new `"agent presets" => { offer_agent_presets_fix(reader, writer, home, config_path)?; }` arm to offer_fixes()'s match statement (~line 497-510), mirroring the existing "config"/"allowed roots" arms exactly -- offer_fixes dispatches purely by check.label string match, not by ok/critical, so no new Check variant/constructor is needed.

3. Write `pub(super) fn offer_agent_presets_fix(reader: &mut impl BufRead, writer: &mut impl Write, home: &Path, config_path: &Path) -> io::Result<bool>` following offer_config_fix's own documented pattern (~line 515-521: state is re-derived from the file itself rather than trusting the phase-1 marker): re-load `Config::load_file(config_path)`; if it fails to load or its agent_presets is non-empty (per D3 -- ANY existing entries, even one, means do nothing), return Ok(false) without prompting. If empty, prompt via `prompt::confirm(reader, writer, "agent_presets is empty -- seed the default presets now?", true)`; decline returns Ok(false). On confirm (per D2 -- reuse, never hand-copy): call `default_config_json(home)` (already in this file, ~line 902) to get the canonical default document, parse it with `serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>`, pull its "agent_presets" value; parse the current raw file content the same way, splice that agent_presets value into the current document's "agent_presets" key, reserialize with `serde_json::to_string_pretty`, persist via `crate::config::write::persist_validated(config_path, &candidate_json)` -- never a hand-written JSON literal for the presets. Report success/failure to `writer` the same way offer_config_fix does.

4. Add tests to the existing `mod tests` block reusing the exact reader()/tempdir harness already used by config_fix_creates_missing_config and config_fix_repairs_empty_allowed_roots_via_added_root: exactly these 3 named test functions --
   - `agent_presets_check_is_never_critical` -- build a Check for a config with empty agent_presets and assert ok==true, critical==false, matching check_constructors_carry_intent's style.
   - `agent_presets_fix_skips_when_already_populated` -- write a config.json with 1 preset already present, call offer_agent_presets_fix, assert it returns Ok(false), assert the file bytes are unchanged, and assert no prompt text was written (empty writer).
   - `agent_presets_fix_seeds_from_default_config_json_when_empty` -- write a config.json with `"agent_presets":[]`, feed reader("y\n"), call offer_agent_presets_fix, assert Ok(true), reload the file with Config::load_file and assert its agent_presets equals Config::load_str(&default_config_json(home)).unwrap().agent_presets, and assert an unrelated existing field (e.g. herdr_session) is preserved unchanged.

truths: offer_agent_presets_fix returns Ok(false) and writes nothing when the config's agent_presets already has 1+ entries
offer_agent_presets_fix seeds agent_presets by parsing default_config_json's own output, never a separately hand-written JSON/preset literal in the new code
the new 'agent presets' check is always Check::info (ok:true), never Check::fail or critical:true
persisting the seeded config preserves every other existing field unchanged
artifacts: src/doctor/checks.rs contains a function named offer_agent_presets_fix
src/doctor/checks.rs's offer_fixes match statement contains an "agent presets" arm calling offer_agent_presets_fix
src/doctor/checks.rs's mod tests contains agent_presets_check_is_never_critical, agent_presets_fix_skips_when_already_populated, and agent_presets_fix_seeds_from_default_config_json_when_empty
key_links: per D1, D2, D3, D4 in docs/history/seed-agent-presets-legacy-config/CONTEXT.md
prohibitions: never mark the new check critical:true / Check::fail
never modify allowed_roots or any other existing config field when persisting the seeded agent_presets
never hand-write a new JSON literal containing preset labels/argv -- must be parsed from default_config_json's own output
never create a new src/config/write.rs helper -- reuse persist_validated as-is

verify: grep -q 'fn offer_agent_presets_fix' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q '"agent presets" =>' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'fn agent_presets_check_is_never_critical' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'fn agent_presets_fix_skips_when_already_populated' src/doctor/checks.rs && grep -q 'fn agent_presets_fix_seeds_from_default_config_json_when_empty' src/doctor/checks.rs && cargo test --quiet doctor::checks

trace.outcome: Added the 'agent presets' info check and offer_agent_presets_fix guided fix, seeded from default_config_json's own output; 3 new unit tests
trace.verify_output: All 5 grep checks passed; cargo test --quiet doctor::checks: running 20 tests / .................... / test result: ok. 20 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 295 filtered out
